text: The System shall operate with Microsoft Internet Explorer versions 5.0 and 6.0.
The System shall operate with Netscape Communicator version 4.7.
The System shall operate with Netscape versions 6 and 7.
The System shall operate with FireFox.
The System shall operate on Certified and Accredited servers running corporate approved operating systems.
The Data Management component shall permit user access from the corporate Intranet.
The Data Management component shall permit authorized user access through the corporate firewall from an Internet connection.
The Data Research and Reporting component shall provide public access to reviewed and approved data sets.
The System shall use the current corporate standard Microsoft SQL Server database engine.
The System shall be in compliance with all applicable Accessibility, Web Design, and Security policies.
The System shall provide a tutorial that includes a quick start and a walk-thru of major system features.
The System shall provide an online hierarchical and cross-linked help system in HTML format.
The System shall provide an online form to enable users to request help.
The System shall provide a user's guide containing instructions required to access and use the data system.
The Database shall house collective data assembled or generated during research activities including spatial and tabular data.
The System shall provide a top-level screen to navigate and select individual watershed sites and their data.
The System shall provide access to summary descriptions of research watersheds, stations, and instruments.
The System shall provide tools to browse, query, and download individual sampling station data and metadata.
The System shall provide charting tools to visualize time-series data over user-specified time frames.
The System shall generate tabular reports of selected data.
The System shall provide web-based geographic information tools to view, query, and download spatial data.
The System shall allow users to examine, visualize, and download agricultural modeling data and results.
The System shall maintain a metadata database that provides browse and query access to formal descriptions of database elements.
The System shall provide tools for database contributors to create and upload metadata.
The Metadata query tools shall support searches by location, theme, and keyword.
The System shall store hundreds of megabytes of data on demand.
The System shall perform week-nightly backups of all user data, application data, and configuration files.
The System shall perform weekly off-site storage of backups with 24-hour retrieval times.
The Application and Database servers shall be load balanced at the application level.
The System shall use a back-end private network for database backups and large data transfers.
The System shall provide data in standardized formats including tab-delimited text.
The System shall support multiple levels of time-series data aggregation including daily, monthly, and yearly.
The User Interface shall use terminology commonly understood by the intended users.
The System shall display a warning message when a low transmission speed is detected.
The System shall provide a non-graphical interface option.
The metadata query response time shall be three seconds or less.
The System shall be available to the intended audience 24 hours per day, 7 days a week.
The System shall maintain 99% availability with a minimum tolerance of 50% of working hours in any week.
The System shall use single-sign-on systems for user authentication.
The System shall require user-id and password authentication for data modification and access to protected data.
The System shall display descriptive and user-friendly messages for invalid input.
The System shall display confirmation messages upon successful data processing.
The Web pages shall be designed for a minimum screen resolution of 800x600 pixels.
The System shall require cookies to be enabled in the browser settings for proper functionality.
